purpose: Embedded swarm builder/editor view used to compose initialization.json before launching a swarm. Manages swarmName, swarmType (Waves|Chains toggle), agents list, and waves list. Tasks are grouped by wave and sorted by wave id. Add/Edit opens TaskEditorModal which calls back with task data; new tasks auto-resolve a wave (existing waves[0], explicit `_newWaveName`, or default 'Wave 1') and assign sub-ids `{waveId}.{nextSub}`. Delete cascades by removing the agent and stripping its id from all `depends_on` arrays. Launch composes a final initData payload (task metadata, agents, waves, empty chains+history) and invokes onLaunch.

exports.name: SwarmBuilder
exports.signature: { onLaunch: (initData) => void, onCancel?: () => void, initData?: object, dashboardId: string }

gotchas: Task IDs are generated as `{wave}.{subIndex}` — if a user reorders waves or creates a new wave with the same id as a deleted one, ids can collide.
Chains array is hard-coded as `[]` in the launch payload — even when swarmType==='Chains', SwarmBuilder produces no chain definitions; the consumer must derive chains downstream.
recalcWaves DROPS waves whose total === 0 — a user creating an empty wave has it removed on the next agent change.
Launch is disabled only on empty name OR zero agents — does not validate dependency cycles or duplicate ids.
patterns: recalcWaves(agentList, waveList) is called everywhere the agents change to keep wave counts in sync and prune empty waves.
Modal state lives in this component (taskEditorOpen + editingTask) and the modal is conditionally mounted only when open.
conventions: Default Wave 1 created when first agent is added with no existing waves.
_newWaveName is a temporary marker passed through TaskEditorModal — stripped before persisting.
